signals.title: USD — Fed Stance + Inflation + Yield Curve
signals.text: US May PCE inflation topped 4.1% (vs 4.1% expected), reinforcing NY Fed Williams's remarks on delaying the 2% inflation target to 2028 while maintaining a 'higher-for-longer' policy stance. Despite today's DXY -0.15% weakness, the underlying inflation pressure and Fed's resolve suggest continued USD support. The 2s10s yield curve at +70.2bp (BULL STEEPENER) indicates recession pricing, which typically favors safe-haven USD. Watch for further Fed commentary on inflation trajectory.

signals.title: NZD/USD — G10 Bilateral Theme + COT + RBNZ
signals.text: NZD is the weakest G10 currency this week, averaging -1.502% against peers, while USD is the strongest at +0.767%, creating a 2.269% spread. This divergence is amplified by NZD/USD COT LF net short -22,454 (long%=18.4%), indicating extreme speculative short positioning against an RBNZ with an active hike bias. If RBNZ delivers or signals a hike at the next meeting, a crowded spec short unwind risk is elevated. NZD/USD at 0.5647. The next RBNZ meeting (mkt: 83% hike priced) on 8 Jul is the key catalyst.

signals.title: USD/JPY — Intervention Risk + Carry + Vol
signals.text: USD/JPY at 161.76 continues to test levels above 160.00, increasing BoJ intervention risk. The 264bp SOFR-TONA carry premium strongly favors USD, sustaining the bid despite the BoJ's recent hike. Options market shows elevated vol premium with IV 7.2% vs HV30 2.3% (VRP +4.9%), indicating strong hedging demand for JPY weakness. JPY COT LF net short -112,092 (long%=29.7%) is a crowded carry trade. A sustained break above 162.00 is likely to trigger official BoJ action; watch for any verbal warnings.

signals.title: AUD/USD — Positioning + Vol + CESI
signals.text: AUD/USD COT LF net long +38,548 (long%=70.2%) indicates significant speculative bullish positioning. However, the pair faces headwinds from a negative CESI score for AUD (net -7 misses), suggesting disappointing economic data. Options market shows extreme vol premium with IV 16.0% vs HV30 8.2% (VRP +7.8%), signaling strong hedging demand for AUD downside protection. AUD/USD at 0.6910. Watch for RBA commentary on economic data to confirm or invalidate the current positioning.
